content:
I discussed with Jensen Huang how top AI models like Claude and Gemini are trained on Google’s TPU, questioning Nvidia's competitive edge. He responded with confidence in Nvidia’s dominance, emphasizing performance, total cost of ownership, and the challenges rivals face.

• Nvidia’s computing stack delivers the best performance per total cost of ownership, unmatched by alternatives.
• Jensen challenges competitors by highlighting that InferenceMax is available for all, yet TPU and Trainium aren’t gaining traction.
• He welcomes experimentation with other hardware, believing it ultimately proves Nvidia’s superiority.
• Creating a better ASIC than Nvidia is extremely difficult, and many attempts have been canceled despite big claims.
